circular.title: Master Direction - Know Your Customer (KYC) Direction, 2016
circular.summary: The Master Direction aims to prevent regulated entities from being used for money laundering or terrorist financing activities. It outlines the requirements for customer acceptance policy, risk management, customer identification procedure, and ongoing due diligence. The direction applies to all regulated entities of the Reserve Bank of India.

clauses.text: To prevent REs from being used, intentionally or unintentionally, by criminal elements for money laundering or terrorist financing activities.
"Aadhaar number" refers to the resident identification number issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI).
"Customer" means a person who is engaged in a financial transaction or activity with a Regulated Entity (RE).
No account is opened in anonymous or fictitious/benami name.
No account is opened where the RE is unable to apply appropriate Customer Due Diligence (CDD) measures.
REs shall put in place a Board approved Risk based Approach (RBA) for KYC.
High risk customers must be reviewed at least once every two years.
clauses.summary: Review requirement for high risk customers

clauses.text: Non-compliance with risk categorisation review may attract a daily penalty of ₹5,000 after 30 days of the deadline.
REs shall undertake identification of customers while establishing an account-based relationship.
The Aadhaar authentication process must be kept secure.
REs shall undertake on-going due diligence of customers to ensure that their transactions are consistent with their knowledge about the customers.

obligations.text: REs shall put in place a Board approved Risk based Approach (RBA) for KYC.
obligations.plain_english: Regulated entities must implement a risk-based approach for KYC

obligations.text: High risk customers must be reviewed at least once every two years.
obligations.plain_english: High risk customers must be reviewed every two years

obligations.text: REs shall undertake identification of customers while establishing an account-based relationship.
obligations.plain_english: Regulated entities must identify customers when opening an account

obligations.text: The Aadhaar authentication process must be kept secure.
obligations.plain_english: Regulated entities must keep the Aadhaar authentication process secure

obligations.text: REs shall undertake on-going due diligence of customers to ensure that their transactions are consistent with their knowledge about the customers.
obligations.plain_english: Regulated entities must conduct ongoing due diligence on customers

prohibitions.text: No account is opened in anonymous or fictitious/benami name.
prohibitions.plain_english: Regulated entities cannot open anonymous accounts

prohibitions.text: No account is opened where the RE is unable to apply appropriate Customer Due Diligence (CDD) measures.
prohibitions.plain_english: Regulated entities cannot open accounts without CDD

definitions.definition: resident identification number issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI)
definitions.plain_english: Unique identification number issued by the government

definitions.definition: person who is engaged in a financial transaction or activity with a Regulated Entity (RE)
definitions.plain_english: Person who does business with a regulated entity
